competitions.title: GENIUS Olympiad 2026
competitions.shortDesc: An international high school competition focused on environmental issues across various disciplines.
competitions.fullDetails: GENIUS Olympiad is an international high school competition hosted in New York, where every project must connect to the environment in some way. Categories include science, engineering, robotics, business, art, short film, music, creative writing, and, new for 2026, AI. Approximately 50% of submissions are accepted as finalists, and roughly 80% of finalists receive some form of medal. The event draws around 1,300 students from over 70 countries each year, with a week of presentations, networking, and optional trips.
competitions.deadline: Typically in early Spring, check website for exact 2026 dates
competitions.location: New York (in-person finals), with regional dates varying.
competitions.prizes: Medals for finalists, with various levels of recognition.
competitions.rounds: Regional competitions leading to national finals.
competitions.fee: $50 application fee, plus $425 if selected for in-person finals.

competitions.title: Regeneron International Science and Engineering Fair (ISEF) 2026
competitions.shortDesc: The world's largest pre-collegiate science research competition for high school students.
competitions.fullDetails: The Regeneron International Science and Engineering Fair (ISEF) is the world's largest and most prestigious pre-college science research competition, bringing together nearly 2,000 high school students who have won regional competitions from around the world. Participants conduct original research projects and present them to judges. ISEF helps students develop advanced research, critical thinking, and presentation skills, with top projects earning global recognition, scholarships, and significant advantages for college and STEM career pathways. Qualification for ISEF happens through affiliated regional and state-level fairs globally.
competitions.deadline: First round (regional fairs) typically Jan/Feb 2026, check local fair websites for detailed dates.
competitions.prizes: Prizes at the international level are in the tens of thousands, including scholarships and global recognition.
competitions.rounds: Multiple rounds, starting with regional/state fairs leading to the international competition.
competitions.fee: Varies by regional fair; typically free to enter regional events that qualify for ISEF.

competitions.title: 2026 International Youth Competition
competitions.shortDesc: An international competition inviting students to share ideas and perspectives on social challenges through visual art, essays, or open submissions.
competitions.fullDetails: The 2026 International Youth Competition strives for greater interest and participation in social challenges from students around the world. The theme for 2026 is 'Why Me?'. Students can submit entries in Visual Art (drawings, illustrations, paintings, photos), Essay (persuasive or creative written works in any language with English translation), or an Open category for other creative ideas. The competition is open to primary, middle, and high school students, as well as college students, as individuals or teams. Judging is based on message, idea, originality, and delivery.
competitions.interest: visual art, essay writing, social challenges
competitions.eventDate: Winners announced by end of September 2026.
competitions.prizes: Awards are given for each category in each division (Primary, Middle School, High School, College). All participants receive a 'Distinguished Achievement' certificate.

competitions.title: Conrad Challenge
competitions.shortDesc: An international competition challenging high school students to develop innovative and entrepreneurial solutions to global challenges.
competitions.fullDetails: The Conrad Challenge is open to high school students aged 13-18 globally. Participants can form teams of up to five, supervised by a mentor. The competition is free to enter and focuses on fostering entrepreneurship and innovation, with no prior experience required. The 2026-2027 round is expected to open for registrations in August 2026.
competitions.eventDate: Varies by stage, summit typically in April
competitions.location: Online (initial stages), potentially in-person summit for finalists
competitions.prizes: Recognition, mentorship, networking opportunities (specific prizes vary)

competitions.title: NCSS Student Leadership & Research Conference
competitions.shortDesc: A conference for high school students to present STEM research, engage in leadership development, and explore entrepreneurship.
competitions.fullDetails: The 2026 NCSS Student Leadership & Research Conference is hosted by the National Consortium of STEM Schools. It aims to build on student research while expanding its focus to include leadership development and entrepreneurship in STEM. Students can present original research, attend leadership sessions, engage in entrepreneurship programming, and interact with active researchers.
competitions.deadline: To be announced (registration details not yet available, but the event is July 16-18, 2026)
competitions.prizes: Opportunity to present research, networking, learning experiences

competitions.title: International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO)
competitions.shortDesc: The World Championship Mathematics Competition for high school students, held annually.
competitions.fullDetails: The International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO) is the most prestigious mathematics competition for high school students globally, attracting participants from over 100 countries. Students must qualify through their respective country's national mathematics Olympiad. The competition features challenging problem-solving rounds.
competitions.deadline: Varies by national qualification pathway (July 2026 for international finals)
competitions.location: To be announced (host country varies annually)
competitions.prizes: Gold, Silver, and Bronze medals, international recognition
competitions.rounds: Multiple (national qualification leading to international finals)
competitions.fee: Free Entry (qualification may have local fees)

competitions.title: Google Science Fair
competitions.shortDesc: A globally recognized online science competition inviting students to solve real-world problems through original research or prototypes.
competitions.fullDetails: The Google Science Fair is open to students aged 13-18 from all countries. Participants identify a real-world problem and design an original research project or prototype to address it, submitting their findings via an online platform. Judges include scientists, engineers, and Google researchers.
competitions.eventDate: To be announced (virtual submission and judging)
competitions.link: https://www.googlesciencefair.com/ (Note: As of May 2026, the specific 2026 page was not live, but mentioned in 2026 competition lists.)

competitions.title: International Economics Olympiad (IEO)
competitions.shortDesc: An international competition designed to challenge and inspire high school students in economics, financial literacy, and business.
competitions.fullDetails: The International Economics Olympiad is a prestigious competition for high school students, focusing on economics, financial literacy, and business. Participants typically qualify through national-level competitions. The international event brings together top students to solve complex economic problems and business cases.
competitions.deadline: Varies by national qualification pathway (August 2026 for international finals)
competitions.location: To be announced (host country varies annually)
competitions.rounds: Multiple (national qualification leading to international finals)
competitions.fee: Free Entry (qualification may have local fees)

competitions.title: International Sustainability Competition
competitions.shortDesc: Challenges high school students to develop original solutions to pressing environmental and sustainability issues.
competitions.fullDetails: This competition invites high school students worldwide to identify a sustainability challenge (e.g., climate, water, food systems, biodiversity, clean energy) and develop an original research-based or innovation-based solution. Submissions include a written report and presentation, evaluated by international judges from academia and industry. The competition is virtual for submissions.
competitions.deadline: To be announced (virtual submission period typically allows for several months)
competitions.link: Not explicitly found, but mentioned as a top international competition in LogoLife Blog

competitions.title: 2026 Global Essay Prize (John Locke Institute)
competitions.shortDesc: A prestigious essay competition encouraging critical thinking across various humanities and social science subjects for students up to 18 years old.
competitions.fullDetails: The John Locke Institute's Global Essay Prize is open to students up to 18 years old worldwide. It challenges participants to write compelling essays on thought-provoking questions across categories like Philosophy, Politics, Economics, History, Psychology, Theology, and Law. It is an online competition with free entry. The deadline for 2026 submissions is May 31, 2026.
competitions.interest: humanities, social sciences, critical thinking, writing

competitions.title: National STEM Festival
competitions.shortDesc: A U.S. competition where students address real-world problems aligned with six challenge themes, with top projects earning a trip to Washington D.C.
competitions.fullDetails: The National STEM Festival is open to U.S. students in grades 7-12. Participants create projects that address real-world problems within themes like Aerospace Innovation, Environmental Stewardship, Future Food, Health & Medicine, Power the Planet, and Tech for Good. Top projects from each U.S. state, D.C., and territories are selected as Challenge Finalists and submit a video for the second round. National Champions are awarded a trip to Washington D.C. in 2026.
competitions.prizes: Trip to Washington D.C., national recognition
competitions.rounds: Multiple (project submission, video round, national champion selection)

competitions.title: Global Impact Competition 2026
competitions.shortDesc: Challenges students worldwide to investigate community issues through field research and policy writing to kickstart meaningful passion projects.
competitions.fullDetails: The Global Impact Competition 2026 calls on high school students from around the world to conduct field research on a community issue and develop policy writing solutions. It's designed for students looking to engage in impactful projects and connect with outstanding peers globally. Potential internship opportunities are available for top achievers.
competitions.prizes: Up to $250 cash prize, potential internship opportunities
competitions.rounds: Multiple stages (field research, policy writing, evaluation)

competitions.title: Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge (GQMC)
competitions.shortDesc: An international, accessible, research-oriented challenge for high school and university students interested in the quantum world.
competitions.fullDetails: The Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge (GQMC) is an international competition aimed at high school and university students interested in quantum mechanics. It provides an accessible and research-oriented platform for learning about the quantum world. Top achievers in the final round receive cash prizes.
competitions.prizes: 1st Prize Senior: 250 USD, 2nd Prize Senior: 175 USD
competitions.fee: To be announced (initial image submission free)

competitions.title: 67th International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO) 2026
competitions.shortDesc: The world's most prestigious mathematics competition for high school students, featuring challenging proof-based problems.
competitions.fullDetails: The IMO is an annual global competition designed for high school students with a strong interest in problem-solving and logical thinking. Students are selected through rigorous national-level processes in their respective countries. The competition evaluates mathematical creativity and precision, and encourages international collaboration among young mathematicians.
competitions.deadline: Final student details submission by April 29, 2026; full payment of fees by May 29, 2026.
competitions.prizes: Gold, Silver, and Bronze medals, national recognition.
competitions.rounds: National selection process, followed by the international competition.
competitions.fee: Free to participate after national qualification (participation fees covered by national bodies).

competitions.title: VEX Robotics World Championship 2026 (High School V5)
competitions.shortDesc: An international robotics competition where high school teams design, build, and program robots to compete in a specific game.
competitions.fullDetails: The VEX Robotics World Championship is an annual event that brings together top VEX V5 Robotics Competition teams from around the globe. Teams compete in the 2025-2026 game, 'Push Back,' aiming to be crowned champions. In addition to the main competition, the event features skills challenges, workshops, and exhibitions, fostering engagement with STEM-related content and encouraging teamwork and problem-solving.
competitions.deadline: Qualification for this championship occurred through regional/national events during the 2025-2026 season.
competitions.location: America's Center, St. Louis, Missouri, USA
competitions.prizes: Championship titles, trophies, recognition, and networking opportunities with universities and industry.
competitions.rounds: Regional/national qualifying events, followed by the World Championship.
competitions.fee: Varies by regional event and team registration; travel and lodging costs apply for the championship.

competitions.title: Zero Robotics 2026 High School Tournament: Galactic Greenhouse
competitions.shortDesc: A unique challenge where high school students program NASA's Astrobee robots onboard the International Space Station (ISS).
competitions.fullDetails: Zero Robotics is an educational program that hosts tournaments to teach middle and high school students to write code and program with robots. The 2026 High School Tournament, themed 'Galactic Greenhouse,' tasks students with programming NASA's Astrobee robots to manage crops in microgravity conditions on the ISS. This program offers an unparalleled opportunity to engage with space robotics and advanced coding in a real-world space environment.
competitions.deadline: Registration is open for the 2026 tournament.
competitions.eventDate: 2026 (specific tournament dates not yet detailed, but the program runs in 2026).
competitions.location: Online programming; robots operate on the International Space Station (ISS).
competitions.prizes: Educational experience, recognition for successful programs, engagement with NASA technology.
competitions.fee: Not specified (educational programs can be free or have a fee).

competitions.title: IEEE Computer Society Global Student Challenge (GSC) 2026
competitions.shortDesc: An international challenge for students to develop innovative solutions to programming and data analytic problems.
competitions.fullDetails: The IEEE Computer Society Global Student Challenge 2026 invites student teams to create innovative solutions for real-world programming and data analytic problems. Teams of two work on challenge prompts related to areas like Backdoor Attacks in Distributed ML, Vulnerabilities in Github CI/CD Workflows, and Analyzing Resource Usage in Data Centers. The competition involves virtual submission for Phase I and in-person presentations for finalists in Phase II.
competitions.eventDate: Phase I: July 13 - August 9, 2026 (Virtual); Phase II (Finals): December 11-14, 2026
competitions.location: Phase I: Online; Phase II: Los Angeles, California, USA
competitions.prizes: Total prize pool of US$5,000 among top 5 teams, plus 1 year of IEEE and IEEE Computer Society Student Membership.

competitions.title: Global Challenge Weekend
competitions.shortDesc: An intensive online weekend experience for high school students to address global challenges aligned with UN Sustainable Development Goals.
competitions.fullDetails: High school students from across the world meet online to brainstorm possibilities, research options, pitch ideas, develop prototypes, and present their projects. Teams of 3-5 students collaborate over 36 hours with faculty mentors available for guidance. Topics bridge STEM and humanities.
competitions.interest: STEM, Humanities, UN SDGs, Innovation, Problem-Solving, Design Thinking
competitions.deadline: Registration Open (check official website for final deadline)
competitions.fee: Not specified, check official website for details.

competitions.title: Oxford Saïd Global Climate Tech Challenge
competitions.shortDesc: High school students and teachers worldwide develop tech-driven solutions to the climate crisis.
competitions.fullDetails: This free-to-enter competition invites participants to use technology, creativity, and problem-solving skills to generate solutions and lesson plans for global or local climate challenges, such as air pollution, extreme weather, food security, and water scarcity. Shortlisted student teams and their teachers receive an all-expenses-paid trip to Oxford in August.
competitions.interest: Climate Change, Technology, Innovation, Environmental Science, Entrepreneurship
competitions.deadline: Not specified, check official site for 2026 deadlines.
competitions.prizes: Full scholarship to the Future Climate Tech Innovators summer school for the winning student team and a place on an Oxford Executive Education program for the winning teacher.

dailyInspiration.title: Maryam Mirzakhani: From IMO Gold to Fields Medal
dailyInspiration.story: Maryam Mirzakhani, an Iranian mathematician, distinguished herself by winning gold medals at the International Mathematical Olympiad in both 1994 and 1995, even achieving a perfect score in her second win. Her early success in these rigorous competitions honed her problem-solving skills and paved the way for an illustrious academic career. Ultimately, Mirzakhani made history as the first woman to be awarded the Fields Medal, often considered the Nobel Prize of mathematics, for her groundbreaking work on Riemann surfaces. Her journey stands as a powerful testament to the impact of dedication and intellectual curiosity in breaking barriers in STEM fields.
dailyInspiration.takeaway: Dedication and intellectual curiosity can pave the way for groundbreaking achievements and break barriers.
